what_it_does: Implements an in-memory, process-cached table of binary-discovered capabilities (CapabilityEntry rows) for one or more platforms, with dict-like lookup, search, snapshot, and module-level singleton accessors.
observations: Entries are keyed by full nested id <platform>/<kind>/<name> so multiple platforms can coexist without collision.
discover() merges rows for a platform rather than replacing the whole registry and stamps each entry with its platform_id.
Dict-like access accepts both full nested ids and legacy leaf ids <kind>/<name>, falling back to a hardcoded default platform 'claude-code'.
A module-level singleton (_registry_singleton) is installed by set_capability_registry and reached by capability_registry().
capability_registry() raises RuntimeError with a long explanatory message if the singleton has not been set.
snapshot() delegates projection to introspection.engine.project and adds platform_id/version metadata.
search() filters results by e.verbs.searchable and case-insensitive substring match over id/name/description.
The engine is imported lazily inside discover() and snapshot() to avoid adapter imports at module load time.
standouts: Deliberate cached-singleton divergence from sibling registries, explicitly defended in comments and error text to avoid repeated expensive binary discovery.
Multi-platform merge with full nested keys plus a backward-compatible legacy leaf-id fallback.
Dict-like container interface (__getitem__, __contains__, __iter__, __len__, get) backed by a private _key resolver.
conventions: Follows a dict-like container contract for the capability table.
Follows lazy import of the engine to keep module load side-effect-free.
Follows fail-loud behavior when the singleton accessor is called before installation.
Breaks the sibling fresh-discover-on-each-call registry pattern intentionally and documents the divergence.
Relies on an external refresh flow (LANE-REFRESH) for on-disk cache/version-stamp writes rather than writing here.
concerns: Legacy default platform 'claude-code' is hardcoded in _key, so legacy leaf lookups silently depend on that platform being present.
capability_registry() embeds detailed references to Suite.__init__ and sibling patterns in its error message, coupling error text to external wiring.
discover() mutates self and returns self, making chained usage side-effectful.
search() assumes every CapabilityEntry has a verbs attribute with a searchable boolean; a malformed entry would raise AttributeError.
notes: This is the cached, process-wide capability leaf table that faces resolve through a singleton accessor, with explicit documentation of why it diverges from fresh-discover siblings.
questions: What is the exact shape of CapabilityEntry and its verbs attribute, and where is it defined?
How and when does Suite.__init__ build, discover, and install the registry, and when is it rebuilt?
What keys and schema does engine.project return in snapshot() beyond platform_id and version?
What is the intended thread-safety model for the module-level singleton?
How should legacy leaf ids behave when the default platform 'claude-code' has not been discovered?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is an in-memory table of binary-discovered capabilities; the code matches and adds multi-platform merge plus legacy-id fallback.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a single, cheap-to-access source of truth for capability metadata discovered from live binaries, while preserving old cap:// lookup semantics and accommodating multiple platforms without key collisions.
novelty: The deliberate cached-singleton divergence from fresh-discover sibling registries, explicitly defended in comments and error messages, plus full-nested multi-platform keys with a legacy leaf fallback.
contribution: A process-wide, in-memory capability table with dict-like access, multi-platform keying, search/snapshot helpers, and a documented singleton accessor that avoids repeated binary subprocess discovery.
summary_for_embedding: CapabilityRegistry is an in-memory, cached module-level singleton table holding binary-discovered CapabilityEntry rows keyed by full nested platform/kind/name identifiers, exposing dict-like lookup, search, by-kind filtering, and a snapshot projection.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single, discover-once, process-wide source of capability metadata for cap:// resolvers and other faces, while supporting legacy leaf ids and multiple platforms.

symbols.does: In-memory table of discovered capabilities for one or more platforms.
symbols.description: Holds CapabilityEntry rows keyed by full nested id, supports dict-like access with a legacy leaf-id fallback to a default platform, and provides discover, snapshot, search, and by_kind operations.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty entry store and platform/version tracking.
symbols.description: Creates an empty entries dictionary, clears platform_id and version, and initializes the set of known platforms.

symbols.does: Discovers and classifies a platform's capabilities and merges them into the registry.
symbols.description: Lazily imports introspection.engine.classify_platform, stamps each returned entry with the platform id, stores them under full nested keys, updates the last platform_id and known platforms set, and returns self.

symbols.does: Returns a face-neutral projection of held rows plus platform/version metadata.
symbols.description: Delegates to engine.project over all stored entries and adds platform_id and version keys for consumers such as the MCP tool or bridge route.
Returns a list of all CapabilityEntry rows whose kind attribute equals the requested kind string.

symbols.does: Searches entries by substring while respecting the searchable verb flag.
symbols.description: Performs a case-insensitive substring match over entry id, name, and description, but only includes entries whose verbs.searchable flag is true.

symbols.does: Retrieves an entry by full or legacy leaf id, raising KeyError on miss.
symbols.description: Resolves the requested id through _key, which accepts full nested ids and legacy leaf ids mapped to the default platform, then returns the matching CapabilityEntry.

symbols.does: Tests whether an entry id is present using full or legacy resolution.
symbols.description: Returns true if _key finds a matching stored key, otherwise false.

symbols.does: Iterates over stored full nested keys.
symbols.description: Returns an iterator over the entries dictionary keys.

symbols.does: Returns the number of held entries.
symbols.description: Reports the count of CapabilityEntry rows currently stored.

symbols.does: Looks up an entry by full or legacy leaf id with an optional default.
symbols.description: Resolves the id via _key and returns the entry if found, otherwise the supplied default.
Assigns the provided registry to the module-level _registry_singleton; intended to be called by Suite.__init__ after discovery, though idempotent replacement is allowed.

symbols.does: Accessor for the cached CapabilityRegistry, failing loud if unset.
symbols.description: Returns the module-level singleton registry or raises RuntimeError with a detailed message explaining the cached-singleton pattern and the required Suite init wiring.
